periodic.operating_state: Revenue reached $35.1 billion, up 94% year-over-year and 17% sequentially, driven by Data Center platforms generating 112% year-over-year growth from Hopper architecture demand for large language model training and inference. Cloud service providers represented approximately 50% of Data Center revenue with strong growth across all customer types. Gaming revenue increased while Professional Visualization and Automotive segments showed modest growth. The company continues transitioning from Hopper to Blackwell architecture with initial Blackwell shipments beginning in Q4.
periodic.cost_structure: Gross margin decreased sequentially to 73.0% from 75.1% due to mix shift to more complex Data Center systems and inventory provisions for low-yielding Blackwell material, though remained above prior year driven by favorable Data Center product mix. Operating expenses increased 44% year-over-year driven by employee expansion, compute infrastructure costs, and engineering development costs for accelerated product roadmap transitions.
periodic.material_events: Board approved additional $50 billion share repurchase authorization and appointed Ellen Ochoa as new director. Company repurchased $11.1 billion of shares in Q3 and $26.2 billion in first nine months of fiscal 2025. Completed successful mask change for Blackwell architecture improving production yields. NVIDIA transitioned certain testing, validation, and supply operations out of China and Hong Kong following export control implementations.
periodic.macro_exposures: USG export controls continue restricting shipments of high-performance products including A100, H100, and Blackwell systems to China and other specified regions, with no licenses received through the NAC process. Geopolitical conflict in Israel affects approximately 4,300 employees primarily supporting networking product development, with substantial numbers called for military duty potentially disrupting operations. Supply chain constraints affect both Hopper and Blackwell systems with critical suppliers and manufacturing partners concentrated in Taiwan.
periodic.forward_outlook: Blackwell production shipments scheduled to begin Q4 fiscal 2025 with continued ramp into fiscal 2026, though demand expected to exceed supply for several quarters. Company implementing annual Data Center architecture cadence with greater product variety to meet diverse AI opportunities while expanding supply chain capacity through prepaid manufacturing agreements and new supplier relationships.
periodic.uncertainty_profile: Regulatory inquiries from multiple jurisdictions including EU, US, UK, China, and South Korea regarding GPU sales, supply allocation, and AI partnerships create ongoing compliance burdens with potential new legislation imposing additional operational restrictions. Complex product transitions requiring simultaneous shipping of multiple architectures create demand prediction challenges and inventory management complexity. Export control rules remain subject to frequent changes with potential expansion to broader product categories and geographic scope.

periodic.operating_state: Total revenues increased 2% to $25.5 billion in Q2 2024, driven by energy storage revenue doubling from increased Megapack deployments while automotive sales revenue declined 9% due to lower average selling prices from price reductions and attractive financing options, partially offset by Cybertruck production ramp. Regulatory credits revenue surged 216% to $890 million as other manufacturers scaled back electric vehicle plans.
periodic.cost_structure: Total gross margin compressed to 18.0% from 18.2% year-over-year as automotive gross margin declined to 18.5% from 19.2% due to lower average selling prices and temporary manufacturing capacity under-utilization during production ramps, partially offset by lower raw material costs and higher IRA manufacturing credits. Energy storage gross margin expanded significantly to 24.6% from 18.4% due to increased IRA credits and higher-margin business mix.
periodic.material_events: Tesla completed global restructuring in Q2 2024 reducing headcount by over 10%, recognizing $583 million in employee termination expenses. Andrew Baglino, Senior Vice President of Powertrain and Energy Engineering, resigned effective April 14, 2024 after 18 years with the company. Stockholders approved redomestication from Delaware to Texas and ratified Elon Musk's 2018 performance-based stock option award at the June 2024 annual meeting.
periodic.macro_exposures: Operations face heightened sensitivity to political and regulatory uncertainty affecting trade and environmental policies, compounded by inflationary pressures and central bank interest rate increases impacting vehicle financing affordability. Foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations on significant intercompany balances created unfavorable impacts of $308 million in Q2 without typical hedging protection. Red Sea shipping conflicts and geopolitical tensions disrupted production at Gigafactory Berlin-Brandenburg through supply chain diversions.
periodic.forward_outlook: Management expects capital expenditures to exceed $10.0 billion in 2024 and range between $8.0-$10.0 billion annually in following two fiscal years for global factory expansion, AI infrastructure, and next-generation platform development. Production growth depends on continued factory ramps, autonomy advances, and new product introductions including robotaxi capabilities while expanding Supercharger network capacity to accommodate NACS adoption by other manufacturers.
periodic.uncertainty_profile: Manufacturing ramp execution remains subject to new product and technology introduction complexities, concurrent international project management, and potential industry-wide component constraints. Energy storage deployment timing varies significantly quarter-to-quarter based on project milestones and logistics coordination while supplier liquidity challenges in the North American automotive industry could affect component access and pricing.
